statusSummary: The explicitly approved package-level profiles are Android managed JVM Bouncy Castle ML-DSA-65, macOS CryptoKit ML-DSA-65, and Windows .NET ML-DSA-65 trust-state use. iOS Simulator package coverage exists, but iOS production remains blocked until a package-neutral release-device host runs the provider-backed trust-state tests and records benchmark, lifecycle, and side-channel evidence. All-supported-platform production remains blocked until iOS evidence is approved.

productionProfiles.approvedUse: Package-level trust-state ML-DSA-65 key generation, signing, verification, import, and export through the managed JVM Bouncy Castle fallback when a consuming product explicitly accepts that provider and its remaining risk.
remainingRisk: Release-device Android benchmark evidence is still recommended follow-up evidence.
Independent external provider-internals crypto review is still recommended follow-up evidence.
Consuming repositories still own storage, migration, rollout, telemetry, and release approval.

productionProfiles.approvedUse: Package-level trust-state ML-DSA-65 key generation, signing, verification, public-key import, public-key export, and CryptoKit integrity-checked private-key import/export on macOS release hardware.
remainingRisk: Secure Enclave ML-DSA production use remains unapproved until non-exportable key lifecycle evidence is approved.
Provider-internals side-channel guarantees remain under Apple's provider boundary; this evidence approves the package boundary and API use.
iOS and Windows package-level production profiles remain separate gates.

productionProfiles.approvedUse: Package-level trust-state ML-DSA-65 key generation, signing, verification, public-key import, public-key export, private-key import, and private-key export through .NET System.Security.Cryptography.MLDsa on GitHub Actions Windows runner evidence.
remainingRisk: GitHub Actions hosted-runner timing evidence is accepted for this checkpoint; pinned Windows hardware remains recommended follow-up evidence.
.NET runtime provider internals are outside this package-boundary review.
iOS package-level production profile remains a separate gate.

ciEvidenceProfiles.acceptedEvidenceSource: GitHub Actions windows-latest runner with actions/setup-dotnet 10.0.x
ciEvidenceProfiles.ownerDecision: Hosted GitHub Actions Windows runner evidence is accepted for Windows .NET ML-DSA runtime support, trust-state conformance, and hosted-runner benchmark evidence for this package-level gate.

blockedOn: Apple iOS package-neutral release-device host evidence for provider-backed trust-state vectors, benchmark, lifecycle, and side-channel evidence.
Any Swift or C# fallback approval would need conformance vectors, audit, benchmark, and side-channel review before production use.
Consuming repositories still own deployed storage, migration, rollout, telemetry, and release approval.
iOS Simulator package coverage is not production evidence.
Physical iOS device testing requires a package-neutral host application or equivalent release-device harness because tool-hosted Swift package tests cannot run directly on device destinations.
iOS release-device provider-backed trust-state vectors over all required objects.
iOS release-device benchmark, lifecycle, and side-channel evidence.

doesNotApprove: per-message ML-DSA signatures in the default message hot path
consumer storage, migration, rollout, telemetry, or release approval
